Title: Simultaneous suppression of lignin, tricin and wall-bound phenolic biosynthesis via the expression of monolignol 4-O-methyltransferases in rice

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: OsC4H promoter (rice cinnamate 4-hydroxylase promoter), lignin-biosynthetic/tissue-associated promoter
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Lignin; tricin; wall-bound ferulate; wall-bound p-coumarate; 4-O-methylated ferulic acid; 4-O-methylated sinapic acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Rice aerial tissues/cell walls; soluble methanolic fraction; lignin fraction; arabinoxylan-bound cell wall fraction
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall structure and recalcitrance; impacts biomass saccharification/feedstock quality
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Phenylpropanoid/lignin biosynthesis and grass cell-wall phenolic metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Monolignol biosynthesis; wall-bound hydroxycinnamate incorporation; tricin-associated lignification
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expressed in rice aerial tissues under OsC4H promoter; strong lignin-biosynthetic context, including anther/tapetum-associated expression inferred from promoter discussion
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth; biomass pretreatment with dilute acid or dilute alkali for saccharification assays
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Coniferyl alcohol; sinapyl alcohol; ferulic acid; sinapic acid; luteolin; tricin
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 4-O-methylated monolignols and related phenolics, including 4-O-methylated ferulic acid and 4-O-methylated sinapic acid derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Regiospecific para-O-methylation of monolignols and, with lower/promiscuous activity, other phenolics/flavones
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Side-route competing with native phenylpropanoid/lignin flux
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with target-product branch and diverts flux away from lignin, tricin incorporation, and wall-bound phenolic formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression in rice; RT-PCR; LC-MS metabolite profiling; alkaline hydrolysis and mild acidolysis of cell walls; acetyl bromide lignin assay; thioacidolysis; 2D HSQC NMR; in vitro enzyme assay; saccharification assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of soluble 4-O-methylated ferulic/sinapic acids while strongly reducing lignin deposition, lignin-bound tricin, and wall-bound hydroxycinnamates; improves saccharification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsC4H-driven overexpression of MOMT4 in rice caused major reductions in total lignin, with 60–75% lower G lignin monomers and up to 93% lower S lignin monomers. 2. MOMT4 lines accumulated high levels of soluble 4-O-methylated ferulic acid (about 8–11 µmol/g DW) and 4-O-methylated sinapic acid (about 5–7 µmol/g DW). 3. MOMT4 reduced wall-bound ferulate by up to 50%, reduced arabinoxylan-bound ferulate by 50–60%, lowered tricin levels, and increased glucose release after pretreatment by up to 30%.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolite profiling by LC-MS; no public omics accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Sterility due to defective pollen/anther development; reduced plant height/biomass after asexual propagation; thinner cell walls; possible buildup of toxic phenolics; raw biomass saccharification gains partly masked
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful engineering tool to reduce grass biomass recalcitrance and improve fermentable sugar release; should preferably be deployed with tissue/cell type-specific expression to mitigate sterility and growth penalties; can be considered for bioenergy/feedstock improvement rather than grain production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MOMT4 shows preference for S-type substrates and more strongly suppresses S lignin than MOMT9; also substantially decreases lignin-bound tricin, likely via substrate promiscuity toward flavones/precursors

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: OsC4H promoter (rice cinnamate 4-hydroxylase promoter), lignin-biosynthetic/tissue-associated promoter
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Lignin; tricin; wall-bound ferulate; wall-bound p-coumarate; 4-O-methylated ferulic acid; 4-O-methylated sinapic acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Rice aerial tissues/cell walls; soluble methanolic fraction; lignin fraction; arabinoxylan-bound cell wall fraction
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall structure and recalcitrance; impacts biomass saccharification/feedstock quality
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Phenylpropanoid/lignin biosynthesis and grass cell-wall phenolic metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Monolignol biosynthesis; wall-bound hydroxycinnamate incorporation; tricin-associated lignification
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expressed in rice aerial tissues under OsC4H promoter; strong lignin-biosynthetic context, including anther/tapetum-associated expression inferred from promoter discussion
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth; biomass pretreatment with dilute acid or dilute alkali for saccharification assays
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Coniferyl alcohol; ferulic acid; tricin; chrysoeriol; sinapyl alcohol (lower activity); sinapic acid
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 4-O-methylated monolignols and related phenolics, especially 4-O-methylated ferulic acid derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Regiospecific para-O-methylation of monolignols; also methylates ferulate and some flavones with notable substrate promiscuity
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Side-route competing with native phenylpropanoid/lignin flux
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with target-product branch and diverts flux away from lignin, tricin incorporation, and wall-bound phenolic formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression in rice; RT-PCR; LC-MS metabolite profiling; alkaline hydrolysis and mild acidolysis of cell walls; acetyl bromide lignin assay; thioacidolysis; 2D HSQC NMR; in vitro enzyme assay; saccharification assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of soluble 4-O-methylated ferulic acid while reducing lignin deposition, lignin-bound tricin, and wall-bound hydroxycinnamates; improves saccharification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsC4H-driven overexpression of MOMT9 in rice reduced total lignin, with about 58–66% lower G lignin monomers and about 77% lower S lignin monomers. 2. MOMT9 lines accumulated soluble 4-O-methylated ferulic acid at levels similar to MOMT4 but produced much less 4-O-methylated sinapic acid, consistent with preference for G-type/ferulate substrates. 3. MOMT9 reduced wall-bound ferulate by up to 50%, reduced arabinoxylan-bound ferulate by 50–60%, lowered tricin levels, and increased glucose release after pretreatment by about 10–15%.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolite profiling by LC-MS; no public omics accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Slight dwarfism in primary transformants; sterility due to defective pollen/anther development; reduced biomass after asexual propagation; thinner cell walls; possible buildup of toxic phenolics; limited gains on raw biomass saccharification
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful engineering tool to reduce grass cell-wall recalcitrance, particularly via suppressing ferulate-associated cross-linking; should be expressed in tissue-specific manner to avoid severe reproductive and growth defects
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MOMT9 has a smaller substrate-binding pocket and preferentially methylates G-type/ferulate substrates, explaining weaker effect on S lignin and very low accumulation of 4-O-methylated sinapate in rice
